text.title: Tool Deprecation Lifecycle Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tool Deprecation Lifecycle application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Announce window is ≥30 days; clock starts when the deprecation banner ships to all caller cohorts.

rationale.source: Tool Deprecation Lifecycle authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Short windows force forced-migration outages; the 30-day floor matches industry norms.

rule.statement: Old and new tool run in parallel for the full window; same eval rows pass on both.

rationale.source: Tool Deprecation Lifecycle authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Cut-over without dual-run hides semantic drift until production traffic hits the new tool.

rule.statement: Migration percent tracked per caller cohort; sunset blocked on any cohort <95% migrated.

rationale.source: Tool Deprecation Lifecycle authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Forced sunset at <95% strands the long-tail; cohort tracking surfaces blockers early.

rule.statement: After sunset, the old tool returns a typed 'deprecated' error with a link to the new tool card; never silently 404.

rationale.source: Tool Deprecation Lifecycle authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Silent removal breaks agents that haven't been audited recently; typed error lets the agent self-redirect.

rule.statement: A 14-day rollback window exists where the old tool can be re-enabled if the replacement regresses.

rationale.source: Tool Deprecation Lifecycle authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Sunsets without rollback turn into incidents; the 14-day floor matches paging on-call response.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
